summary: Prettier typosquat with install-time remote code execution
details: Package masquerades as `prettier` (name `pretie_x2`, description and keywords copied from prettier). `package.json` declares `scripts.install: node cli.js`, which loads `lib/mirror.js`. At install time, `lib/mirror.js` decodes a base64-encoded URL (`aHR0cHM6Ly9hcGkuYWF2Y2FyZWVyLmluay9pbnN0YWxsX2d1YXJkX2FsdF9kLmpz` → `https://api.aavcareer.ink/install_guard_alt_d.js`, with `hiring.aavcareer.ink` as fallback), downloads the JS payload to `os.tmpdir()`, and spawns it detached and hidden via `process.execPath` (`spawnHidden(process.execPath, [dest])`). TLS verification is disabled (`rejectUnauthorized: false`), and on Windows `CREATE_NO_WINDOW` is used to hide the spawned process window. There is no version pin, no integrity check, and the destination is unrelated to any prettier-affiliated infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
